summary: Studio‑like soundboard capture of a legendary Fillmore West ’69 show, highlighted by a monster Eleven, scorching Alligator > Caution jam and an unforgettable Dark Star.
review: The recording is a pristine sound‑board source that rivals studio quality, though a few releases suffer from reversed stereo channels and minor patch glitches. Musically the performance is near mythic – the setlist reads like a greatest‑hits marathon with The Eleven, Dark Star, Alligator > Caution, Lovelight and The Other One delivering jaw‑dropping moments that most reviewers call “the best of ’69.”

ai_rating.rationale: Over thirty reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 5 stars, praising both performance and audio; only one low‑rating comment notes technical anomalies, so the combined show (70%) + recording (30%) score is very high.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, studio‑like clarity; full mix with great instrument separation. A few releases have the left/right channels swapped and minor patch/pitch quirks on Dark Star and Alligator jams.

standout_songs: Dark Star > St. Stephen > The Eleven
show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans rave about the seamless segues – Cryptical Envelopment > The Other One > Cryptical into Dark Star, then the marathon Alligator‑>Caution sequence – making the set feel like a perfectly crafted psychedelic suite.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone on Lovelight and his lead throughout the Eleven are repeatedly called “perfect” and “mind‑blowing.”
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is described as “insane,” especially during Stephen and the Eleven, where he locks in with Jerry.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey receive praise for tight interplay; one reviewer notes the left/right channel reversal but otherwise the drum work is praised as powerful.
